They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[granite]]. The [[Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[Leontopodium]]'' is [[Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[River]]s with [[headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[Ise Bay]] of the [[Pacific Ocean]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[granite]]. The [[Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[Leontopodium]]'' is [[Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[River]]s with [[headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[Ise Bay]] of the [[Pacific Ocean]]. They include: * [[Kiso River]] * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ([[Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Mount Amida]]) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|[[Mount Kisokoma|Mt. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[Leontopodium]]'' is [[Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[mountain range]] in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[granite]]. The [[Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[Leontopodium]]'' is [[Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[mountain range]] in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[granite]]. The [[Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[Leontopodium]]'' is [[Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[Mountain]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[Height]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|Note
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|Japanese

|- |1 |[[File:Kyougatake cyuuou Alps 2009-4-30.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kyō (Nagano)|Mount Kyō]] |{{Convert|2,296.25|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |経ヶ岳 |- |2 |[[File:Chausudake and shougigashirayama from kisokoma 2008 9 9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Shōgikashira]] |{{Convert|2,730|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |将棊頭山 |- |3 |[[File:Kisokomagatake from mugikusadake 25 2007 4 29.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kisokoma]] |{{Convert|2,955.95|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |highest in Kiso Mountains
[[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 famous]] |木曽駒ヶ岳 |- |4 |[[File:Hokendake from Senjojiki 1996-9-8.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Hōken]] |{{Convert|2,931|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |Senjōjiki [[Cirque]] |宝剣岳 |- |5 |[[File:Mount Sannosawa from Mount Hoken 1996-09-08.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Sannosawa]] |{{Convert|2,848.48|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |三ノ沢岳 |- |6 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Hinokio]] |{{Convert|2,827.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |檜尾岳 |- |7 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Kumasawa]] |{{Convert|2,778|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |熊沢岳 |- |8 |[[File:Utsugidake from Komaishi 2001-8-28.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Utsugi]] |{{Convert|2,863.71|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous |空木岳 |- |9 |[[File:Minamikomagatake from Kosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Minamikoma]] |{{Convert|2,841|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |南駒ヶ岳 |- |10 |[[File:Kosumoyama from Sengairei 1995-8-16.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kosumo]] |{{Convert|2,613.24|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |300 famous |越百山 |- |11 |[[File:Okunenjodake from Nenjodake 2010-10-11.JPG|50px]] |[[Mount Okunenjō]] |{{Convert|2,303|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |奥念丈岳 |- |12 |[[File:Anpeijiyama from Minamikosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ampeiji]] |{{Convert|2,363.14|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |安平路山 |- |13 |[[File:Surikogiyama from Nagisodake 2001-12-9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Surikogi]] |{{Convert|2,168.52|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |摺古木山 |- |14 |[[File:Enasan from north 2010-9-17.JPG|50px]] |[[Mount Ena]] |{{Convert|2,191|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous{{cite web|url=http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|url-status=live|archive-date=March 27, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220327085751/http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|title=Mount Ena Outdoor Information|language=ja|website=Nakatsugawa City Regional Information - Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[River]]s with [[headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[Ise Bay]] of the [[Pacific Ocean]]. They include: * [[Kiso River]] * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ([[Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Mount Amida]]) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|[[Mount Kisokoma|Mt. Kisokoma]] and Mt. Hōken seen from [[Mount Sannosawa]] File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *[[Japanese Alps]] **[[Hida Mountains]] (Northern Alps) **[[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] (Southern Alps) *[[List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|List of mountains in Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Kiso Mountains]] [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[mountain range]] in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[granite]]. The [[Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[Leontopodium]]'' is [[Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[Mountain]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[Height]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|Note
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|Japanese

|- |1 |[[File:Kyougatake cyuuou Alps 2009-4-30.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kyō (Nagano)|Mount Kyō]] |{{Convert|2,296.25|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |経ヶ岳 |- |2 |[[File:Chausudake and shougigashirayama from kisokoma 2008 9 9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Shōgikashira]] |{{Convert|2,730|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |将棊頭山 |- |3 |[[File:Kisokomagatake from mugikusadake 25 2007 4 29.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kisokoma]] |{{Convert|2,955.95|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |highest in Kiso Mountains
[[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 famous]] |木曽駒ヶ岳 |- |4 |[[File:Hokendake from Senjojiki 1996-9-8.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Hōken]] |{{Convert|2,931|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |Senjōjiki [[Cirque]] |宝剣岳 |- |5 |[[File:Mount Sannosawa from Mount Hoken 1996-09-08.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Sannosawa]] |{{Convert|2,848.48|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |三ノ沢岳 |- |6 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Hinokio]] |{{Convert|2,827.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |檜尾岳 |- |7 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Kumasawa]] |{{Convert|2,778|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |熊沢岳 |- |8 |[[File:Utsugidake from Komaishi 2001-8-28.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Utsugi]] |{{Convert|2,863.71|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous |空木岳 |- |9 |[[File:Minamikomagatake from Kosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Minamikoma]] |{{Convert|2,841|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |南駒ヶ岳 |- |10 |[[File:Kosumoyama from Sengairei 1995-8-16.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kosumo]] |{{Convert|2,613.24|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |300 famous |越百山 |- |11 |[[File:Okunenjodake from Nenjodake 2010-10-11.JPG|50px]] |[[Mount Okunenjō]] |{{Convert|2,303|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |奥念丈岳 |- |12 |[[File:Anpeijiyama from Minamikosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ampeiji]] |{{Convert|2,363.14|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |安平路山 |- |13 |[[File:Surikogiyama from Nagisodake 2001-12-9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Surikogi]] |{{Convert|2,168.52|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |摺古木山 |- |14 |[[File:Enasan from north 2010-9-17.JPG|50px]] |[[Mount Ena]] |{{Convert|2,191|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous{{cite web|url=http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|url-status=live|archive-date=March 27, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220327085751/http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|title=Mount Ena Outdoor Information|language=ja|website=Nakatsugawa City Regional Information - Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[River]]s with [[headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[Ise Bay]] of the [[Pacific Ocean]]. They include: * [[Kiso River]] * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ([[Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Mount Amida]]) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|[[Mount Kisokoma|Mt. Kisokoma]] and Mt. Hōken seen from [[Mount Sannosawa]] File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *[[Japanese Alps]] **[[Hida Mountains]] (Northern Alps) **[[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] (Southern Alps) *[[List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|List of mountains in Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] }} [[sn:Kiso Mountains]] [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[mountain range]] in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[granite]]. The [[Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[Mountain]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[Height]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|Note
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|Japanese

|- |1 |[[File:Kyougatake cyuuou Alps 2009-4-30.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kyō (Nagano)|Mount Kyō]] |{{Convert|2,296.25|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |経ヶ岳 |- |2 |[[File:Chausudake and shougigashirayama from kisokoma 2008 9 9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Shōgikashira]] |{{Convert|2,730|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |将棊頭山 |- |3 |[[File:Kisokomagatake from mugikusadake 25 2007 4 29.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kisokoma]] |{{Convert|2,955.95|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |highest in Kiso Mountains
[[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 famous]] |木曽駒ヶ岳 |- |4 |[[File:Hokendake from Senjojiki 1996-9-8.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Hōken]] |{{Convert|2,931|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |Senjōjiki [[:en:Cirque|Cirque]] |宝剣岳 |- |5 |[[File:Mount Sannosawa from Mount Hoken 1996-09-08.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Sannosawa]] |{{Convert|2,848.48|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |三ノ沢岳 |- |6 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Hinokio]] |{{Convert|2,827.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |檜尾岳 |- |7 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Kumasawa]] |{{Convert|2,778|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |熊沢岳 |- |8 |[[File:Utsugidake from Komaishi 2001-8-28.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Utsugi]] |{{Convert|2,863.71|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous |空木岳 |- |9 |[[File:Minamikomagatake from Kosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Minamikoma]] |{{Convert|2,841|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |南駒ヶ岳 |- |10 |[[File:Kosumoyama from Sengairei 1995-8-16.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kosumo]] |{{Convert|2,613.24|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |300 famous |越百山 |- |11 |[[File:Okunenjodake from Nenjodake 2010-10-11.JPG|50px]] |[[Mount Okunenjō]] |{{Convert|2,303|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |奥念丈岳 |- |12 |[[File:Anpeijiyama from Minamikosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ampeiji]] |{{Convert|2,363.14|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |安平路山 |- |13 |[[File:Surikogiyama from Nagisodake 2001-12-9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Surikogi]] |{{Convert|2,168.52|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |摺古木山 |- |14 |[[File:Enasan from north 2010-9-17.JPG|50px]] |[[Mount Ena]] |{{Convert|2,191|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous{{cite web|url=http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|url-status=live|archive-date=March 27, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220327085751/http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|title=Mount Ena Outdoor Information|language=ja|website=Nakatsugawa City Regional Information - Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[River]]s with [[:en:headwaters|headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[Ise Bay]] of the [[Pacific Ocean]]. They include: * [[Kiso River]] * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ([[Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Mount Amida]]) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|[[Mount Kisokoma|Mt. Kisokoma]] and Mt. Hōken seen from [[Mount Sannosawa]] File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *[[Japanese Alps]] **[[Hida Mountains]] (Northern Alps) **[[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] (Southern Alps) *[[List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|List of mountains in Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] }} [[sn:Kiso Mountains]] [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[mountain range]] in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|Japanese

Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[River]]s with [[:en:headwaters|headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[:en:Ise Bay|Ise Bay]] of the [[Pacific Ocean]]. They include: * [[Kiso River]] * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ([[Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Mount Amida]]) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|[[Mount Kisokoma|Mt. Kisokoma]] and Mt. Hōken seen from [[Mount Sannosawa]] File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[River]]s with [[:en:headwaters|headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[:en:Ise Bay|Ise Bay]] of the [[Pacific Ocean]]. revision.text:
{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[Mountain]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[:en:Height|Height]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|Note
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|Japanese

|- |1 |[[File:Kyougatake cyuuou Alps 2009-4-30.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kyō (Nagano)|Mount Kyō]] |{{Convert|2,296.25|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |経ヶ岳 |- |2 |[[File:Chausudake and shougigashirayama from kisokoma 2008 9 9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Shōgikashira]] |{{Convert|2,730|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |将棊頭山 |- |3 |[[File:Kisokomagatake from mugikusadake 25 2007 4 29.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kisokoma]] |{{Convert|2,955.95|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |highest in Kiso Mountains
[[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 famous]] |木曽駒ヶ岳 |- |4 |[[File:Hokendake from Senjojiki 1996-9-8.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Hōken]] |{{Convert|2,931|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |Senjōjiki [[:en:Cirque|Cirque]] |宝剣岳 |- |5 |[[File:Mount Sannosawa from Mount Hoken 1996-09-08.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Sannosawa]] |{{Convert|2,848.48|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |三ノ沢岳 |- |6 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Hinokio]] |{{Convert|2,827.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |檜尾岳 |- |7 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Kumasawa]] |{{Convert|2,778|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |熊沢岳 |- |8 |[[File:Utsugidake from Komaishi 2001-8-28.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Utsugi]] |{{Convert|2,863.71|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous |空木岳 |- |9 |[[File:Minamikomagatake from Kosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Minamikoma]] |{{Convert|2,841|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |南駒ヶ岳 |- |10 |[[File:Kosumoyama from Sengairei 1995-8-16.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kosumo]] |{{Convert|2,613.24|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |300 famous |越百山 |- |11 |[[File:Okunenjodake from Nenjodake 2010-10-11.JPG|50px]] |[[Mount Okunenjō]] |{{Convert|2,303|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |奥念丈岳 |- |12 |[[File:Anpeijiyama from Minamikosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ampeiji]] |{{Convert|2,363.14|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |安平路山 |- |13 |[[File:Surikogiyama from Nagisodake 2001-12-9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Surikogi]] |{{Convert|2,168.52|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |摺古木山 |- |14 |[[File:Enasan from north 2010-9-17.JPG|50px]] |[[Mount Ena]] |{{Convert|2,191|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous{{cite web|url=http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|url-status=live|archive-date=March 27, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220327085751/http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|title=Mount Ena Outdoor Information|language=ja|website=Nakatsugawa City Regional Information - Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[River]]s with [[:en:headwaters|headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[:en:Ise Bay|Ise Bay]] of the [[Pacific Ocean]]. They include: * [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ([[Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Mount Amida]]) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|[[Mount Kisokoma|Mt. Kisokoma]] and Mt. Hōken seen from [[Mount Sannosawa]] File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *[[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]] **[[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] (Northern Alps) **[[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] (Southern Alps) *[[List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|List of mountains in Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] }} [[sn:Kiso Mountains]] [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|Japanese

{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[Mountain]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[:en:Height|Height]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|Note
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|Japanese

|- |1 |[[File:Kyougatake cyuuou Alps 2009-4-30.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kyō (Nagano)|Mount Kyō]] |{{Convert|2,296.25|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |経ヶ岳 |- |2 |[[File:Chausudake and shougigashirayama from kisokoma 2008 9 9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Shōgikashira]] |{{Convert|2,730|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |将棊頭山 |- |3 |[[File:Kisokomagatake from mugikusadake 25 2007 4 29.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kisokoma]] |{{Convert|2,955.95|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |highest in Kiso Mountains
[[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 famous]] |木曽駒ヶ岳 |- |4 |[[File:Hokendake from Senjojiki 1996-9-8.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Hōken]] |{{Convert|2,931|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |Senjōjiki [[:en:Cirque|Cirque]] |宝剣岳 |- |5 |[[File:Mount Sannosawa from Mount Hoken 1996-09-08.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Sannosawa]] |{{Convert|2,848.48|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |三ノ沢岳 |- |6 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Hinokio]] |{{Convert|2,827.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |檜尾岳 |- |7 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Kumasawa]] |{{Convert|2,778|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |熊沢岳 |- |8 |[[File:Utsugidake from Komaishi 2001-8-28.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Utsugi]] |{{Convert|2,863.71|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous |空木岳 |- |9 |[[File:Minamikomagatake from Kosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Minamikoma]] |{{Convert|2,841|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |南駒ヶ岳 |- |10 |[[File:Kosumoyama from Sengairei 1995-8-16.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kosumo]] |{{Convert|2,613.24|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |300 famous |越百山 |- |11 |[[File:Okunenjodake from Nenjodake 2010-10-11.JPG|50px]] |[[Mount Okunenjō]] |{{Convert|2,303|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |奥念丈岳 |- |12 |[[File:Anpeijiyama from Minamikosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ampeiji]] |{{Convert|2,363.14|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |安平路山 |- |13 |[[File:Surikogiyama from Nagisodake 2001-12-9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Surikogi]] |{{Convert|2,168.52|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |摺古木山 |- |14 |[[File:Enasan from north 2010-9-17.JPG|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Ena|Mount Ena]] |{{Convert|2,191|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous{{cite web|url=http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|url-status=live|archive-date=March 27, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220327085751/http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|title=Mount Ena Outdoor Information|language=ja|website=Nakatsugawa City Regional Information - Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[River]]s with [[:en:headwaters|headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[:en:Ise Bay|Ise Bay]] of the [[Pacific Ocean]]. They include: * [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ([[Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Mount Amida]]) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|[[Mount Kisokoma|Mt. Kisokoma]] and Mt. Hōken seen from [[Mount Sannosawa]] File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *[[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]] **[[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] (Northern Alps) **[[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] (Southern Alps) *[[:en:List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|List of mountains in Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] }} [[sn:Kiso Mountains]] [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[:en:Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[Mountain]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[:en:Height|Height]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|Note
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|Japanese

|- |1 |[[File:Kyougatake cyuuou Alps 2009-4-30.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kyō (Nagano)|Mount Kyō]] |{{Convert|2,296.25|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |経ヶ岳 |- |2 |[[File:Chausudake and shougigashirayama from kisokoma 2008 9 9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Shōgikashira]] |{{Convert|2,730|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |将棊頭山 |- |3 |[[File:Kisokomagatake from mugikusadake 25 2007 4 29.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]] |{{Convert|2,955.95|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |highest in Kiso Mountains
[[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 famous]] |木曽駒ヶ岳 |- |4 |[[File:Hokendake from Senjojiki 1996-9-8.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Hōken|Mount Hōken]] |{{Convert|2,931|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |Senjōjiki [[:en:Cirque|Cirque]] |宝剣岳 |- |5 |[[File:Mount Sannosawa from Mount Hoken 1996-09-08.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Sannosawa|Mount Sannosawa]] |{{Convert|2,848.48|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |三ノ沢岳 |- |6 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Hinokio]] |{{Convert|2,827.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |檜尾岳 |- |7 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Kumasawa]] |{{Convert|2,778|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |熊沢岳 |- |8 |[[File:Utsugidake from Komaishi 2001-8-28.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Utsugi]] |{{Convert|2,863.71|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous |空木岳 |- |9 |[[File:Minamikomagatake from Kosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Minamikoma|Mount Minamikoma]] |{{Convert|2,841|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |南駒ヶ岳 |- |10 |[[File:Kosumoyama from Sengairei 1995-8-16.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kosumo]] |{{Convert|2,613.24|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |300 famous |越百山 |- |11 |[[File:Okunenjodake from Nenjodake 2010-10-11.JPG|50px]] |[[Mount Okunenjō]] |{{Convert|2,303|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |奥念丈岳 |- |12 |[[File:Anpeijiyama from Minamikosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ampeiji]] |{{Convert|2,363.14|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |安平路山 |- |13 |[[File:Surikogiyama from Nagisodake 2001-12-9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Surikogi]] |{{Convert|2,168.52|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |摺古木山 |- |14 |[[File:Enasan from north 2010-9-17.JPG|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Ena|Mount Ena]] |{{Convert|2,191|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous{{cite web|url=http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|url-status=live|archive-date=March 27, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220327085751/http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|title=Mount Ena Outdoor Information|language=ja|website=Nakatsugawa City Regional Information - Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[River]]s with [[:en:headwaters|headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[:en:Ise Bay|Ise Bay]] of the [[Pacific Ocean]]. They include: * [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ([[Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Mount Amida]]) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|[[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mt. Kisokoma]] and Mt. Hōken seen from [[:en:Mount Sannosawa|Mount Sannosawa]] File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *[[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]] **[[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] (Northern Alps) **[[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] (Southern Alps) *[[:en:List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|List of mountains in Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] }} [[sn:Kiso Mountains]] [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[:en:Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

{{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[:en:Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[:en:Mountain|Mountain]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[:en:Height|Height]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|Note
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|Japanese

|- |1 |[[File:Kyougatake cyuuou Alps 2009-4-30.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kyō (Nagano)|Mount Kyō]] |{{Convert|2,296.25|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |経ヶ岳 |- |2 |[[File:Chausudake and shougigashirayama from kisokoma 2008 9 9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Shōgikashira]] |{{Convert|2,730|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |将棊頭山 |- |3 |[[File:Kisokomagatake from mugikusadake 25 2007 4 29.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]] |{{Convert|2,955.95|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |highest in Kiso Mountains
[[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 famous]] |木曽駒ヶ岳 |- |4 |[[File:Hokendake from Senjojiki 1996-9-8.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Hōken|Mount Hōken]] |{{Convert|2,931|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |Senjōjiki [[:en:Cirque|Cirque]] |宝剣岳 |- |5 |[[File:Mount Sannosawa from Mount Hoken 1996-09-08.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Sannosawa|Mount Sannosawa]] |{{Convert|2,848.48|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |三ノ沢岳 |- |6 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Hinokio]] |{{Convert|2,827.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |檜尾岳 |- |7 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Kumasawa]] |{{Convert|2,778|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |熊沢岳 |- |8 |[[File:Utsugidake from Komaishi 2001-8-28.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Utsugi|Mount Utsugi]] |{{Convert|2,863.71|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous |空木岳 |- |9 |[[File:Minamikomagatake from Kosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Minamikoma|Mount Minamikoma]] |{{Convert|2,841|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |南駒ヶ岳 |- |10 |[[File:Kosumoyama from Sengairei 1995-8-16.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kosumo]] |{{Convert|2,613.24|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |300 famous |越百山 |- |11 |[[File:Okunenjodake from Nenjodake 2010-10-11.JPG|50px]] |[[Mount Okunenjō]] |{{Convert|2,303|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |奥念丈岳 |- |12 |[[File:Anpeijiyama from Minamikosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ampeiji]] |{{Convert|2,363.14|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |安平路山 |- |13 |[[File:Surikogiyama from Nagisodake 2001-12-9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Surikogi]] |{{Convert|2,168.52|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |摺古木山 |- |14 |[[File:Enasan from north 2010-9-17.JPG|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Ena|Mount Ena]] |{{Convert|2,191|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous{{cite web|url=http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|url-status=live|archive-date=March 27, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220327085751/http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|title=Mount Ena Outdoor Information|language=ja|website=Nakatsugawa City Regional Information - Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[:en:River|River]]s with [[:en:headwaters|headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[:en:Ise Bay|Ise Bay]] of the [[:en:Pacific Ocean|Pacific Ocean]]. They include: * [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ([[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Mount Amida]]) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|[[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mt. Kisokoma]] and Mt. Hōken seen from [[:en:Mount Sannosawa|Mount Sannosawa]] File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *[[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]] **[[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] (Northern Alps) **[[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] (Southern Alps) *[[:en:List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|List of mountains in Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] }} [[sn:Kiso Mountains]] [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1072062]] Wikidata name: Kiso Mountains [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1072062]] Wikidata name: Kiso Mountains [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[:en:Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[:en:tree line|tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

They include: * [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ([[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Mount Amida]]) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|[[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mt. Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *[[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]] **[[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] (Northern Alps) **[[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] (Southern Alps) *[[:en:List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|List of mountains in Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] }} [[sn:Kiso Mountains]] [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *[[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]] **[[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] (Northern Alps) **[[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] (Southern Alps) *[[:en:List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|List of mountains in Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] }} [[sn:Kiso Mountains]] [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q1072062}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1072062]] Wikidata name: Kiso Mountains [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1072062]] Wikidata name: Kiso Mountains [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[:en:Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[:en:tree line|tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[:en:Mountain|Mountain]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[:en:Height|Height]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|Note
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|Japanese
