Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[:en:River|River]]s with [[:en:headwaters|headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[:en:Ise Bay|Ise Bay]] of the [[:en:Pacific Ocean|Pacific Ocean]]. They include: * [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ([[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Mount Amida]]) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|[[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mt. Kisokoma]] and Mt. Hōken seen from [[:en:Mount Sannosawa|Mount Sannosawa]] File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *[[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]] **[[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] (Northern Alps) **[[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] (Southern Alps) *[[:en:List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|List of mountains in Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] }} [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q1072062}}

revision.text:
[[arz:جبال كيسو]][[bg:Кисо (хребет)]][[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]][[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]][[de:Kiso-Gebirge]][[en:Kiso Mountains]][[eo:Kiso montaro]][[es:Cordillera Kiso]][[eu:Kiso mendikatea]][[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]][[fr:Monts Kiso]][[he:הרי קיסו]][[it:Monti Kiso]][[ja:木曽山脈]][[ka:კისოს მთები]][[ko:기소산맥]][[lld:Crëps de Kiso]][[lt:Kiso kalnai]][[nl:Kisogebergte]][[pl:Góry Kiso]][[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]][[ru:Кисо (хребет)]][[tr:Kiso Dağları]][[uk:Гори Кісо]][[zh:木曾山脈]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1072062]] Wikidata name: Kiso Mountains [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1072062]] Wikidata name: Kiso Mountains [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[:en:Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[:en:tree line|tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[:en:Mountain|Mountain]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|[[:en:Height|Height]]
! style="background-color:#ddd"|Note
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|Japanese

[[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 famous]] |木曽駒ヶ岳 |- |4 |[[File:Hokendake from Senjojiki 1996-9-8.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Hōken|Mount Hōken]] |{{Convert|2,931|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |Senjōjiki [[:en:Cirque|Cirque]] |宝剣岳 |- |5 |[[File:Mount Sannosawa from Mount Hoken 1996-09-08.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Sannosawa|Mount Sannosawa]] |{{Convert|2,848.48|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |三ノ沢岳 |- |6 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Hinokio]] |{{Convert|2,827.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |檜尾岳 |- |7 |[[File:Gthumb.svg|30px]] |[[Mount Kumasawa]] |{{Convert|2,778|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |熊沢岳 |- |8 |[[File:Utsugidake from Komaishi 2001-8-28.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Utsugi|Mount Utsugi]] |{{Convert|2,863.71|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous |空木岳 |- |9 |[[File:Minamikomagatake from Kosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Minamikoma|Mount Minamikoma]] |{{Convert|2,841|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |南駒ヶ岳 |- |10 |[[File:Kosumoyama from Sengairei 1995-8-16.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Kosumo]] |{{Convert|2,613.24|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |300 famous |越百山 |- |11 |[[File:Okunenjodake from Nenjodake 2010-10-11.JPG|50px]] |[[Mount Okunenjō]] |{{Convert|2,303|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |奥念丈岳 |- |12 |[[File:Anpeijiyama from Minamikosumoyama 2008-9-23.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ampeiji]] |{{Convert|2,363.14|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |200 famous |安平路山 |- |13 |[[File:Surikogiyama from Nagisodake 2001-12-9.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Surikogi]] |{{Convert|2,168.52|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |摺古木山 |- |14 |[[File:Enasan from north 2010-9-17.JPG|50px]] |[[:en:Mount Ena|Mount Ena]] |{{Convert|2,191|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} |100 famous{{cite web|url=http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|url-status=live|archive-date=March 27, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220327085751/http://enasan.net/blog/enasan/|title=Mount Ena Outdoor Information|language=ja|website=Nakatsugawa City Regional Information - Enasan Net|access-date=June 27, 2008}} |恵那山 |- |15 |[[File:OokawairiyamaFromUmanose.jpg|50px]] |[[Mount Ōkawairi]] |{{Convert|1,907.74|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} | |大川入山 |} ;Foothills *[[Mount Nenjō]] (念丈岳), {{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== [[:en:River|River]]s with [[:en:headwaters|headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to [[:en:Ise Bay|Ise Bay]] of the [[:en:Pacific Ocean|Pacific Ocean]]. They include: * [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ([[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Mount Amida]]) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|[[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mt. Kisokoma]] and Mt. Hōken seen from [[:en:Mount Sannosawa|Mount Sannosawa]] File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *[[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]] **[[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] (Northern Alps) **[[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] (Southern Alps) *[[:en:List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|List of mountains in Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] }} [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] {{wikidata link|Q1072062}}

The upper part of the mountain range is the [[:en:tree line|tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

{{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[:en:tree line|tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

|tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[:en:Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[:en:tree line|tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[:en:Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]] | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[:en:tree line|tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from [[:en:Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|Mount Senjō]] in the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] | etymology = | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | subdivision1 = {{hlist|[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]]|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]]}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | Nagano|Kiso District]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[:en:tree line|tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

{{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a [[:en:Mountain range|Mountain range]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]] and [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] prefectures in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[:en:tree line|tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the [[:en:Hida Mountains|Hida Mountains]] ("Northern Alps") and the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Akaishi Mountains]] ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the [[:en:Japanese Alps|Japanese Alps]]. == Outline == The mountain range consists of [[:en:Granite|Granite]]. The [[:en:Komagatake Ropeway|Komagatake Ropeway]] is on the east side of [[:en:Mount Kisokoma|Mount Kisokoma]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the [[:en:tree line|tree line]], and a lot of [[:en:alpine plant|alpine plant]]s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''[[:en:Leontopodium|Leontopodium]]'' is [[:en:Endemism|endemic]] around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}}|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano}}}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma}} | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a {{ill|Mountain range|en|Mountain range|WD=Q46831|lt=Mountain range}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano}} and {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}} prefectures in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the {{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains}} ("Northern Alps") and the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the {{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps}}. == Outline == The mountain range consists of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}}. The {{ill|Komagatake Ropeway|en|Komagatake Ropeway|WD=Q6428059|lt=Komagatake Ropeway}} is on the east side of {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the {{ill|tree line|en|tree line|WD=Q207762|lt=tree line}}, and a lot of {{ill|alpine plant|en|alpine plant|WD=Q2582662|lt=alpine plant}}s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''{{ill|Leontopodium|en|Leontopodium|WD=Q1483297|lt=Leontopodium}}'' is {{ill|Endemism|en|Endemism|WD=Q123452|lt=endemic}} around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the {{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains}} ("Northern Alps") and the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the {{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps}}. == Outline == The mountain range consists of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}}. The {{ill|Komagatake Ropeway|en|Komagatake Ropeway|WD=Q6428059|lt=Komagatake Ropeway}} is on the east side of {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the {{ill|tree line|en|tree line|WD=Q207762|lt=tree line}}, and a lot of {{ill|alpine plant|en|alpine plant|WD=Q2582662|lt=alpine plant}}s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''{{ill|Leontopodium|en|Leontopodium|WD=Q1483297|lt=Leontopodium}}'' is {{ill|Endemism|en|Endemism|WD=Q123452|lt=endemic}} around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *{{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps}} **{{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains}} (Northern Alps) **{{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} (Southern Alps) *{{ill|List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|en|List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|WD=Q467843|lt=List of mountains in Japan}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] {{wikidata link|Q1072062}} [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1072062]] Wikidata name: Kiso Mountains [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from {{ill|Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|en|Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|WD=Q3695490|lt=Mount Senjō}} in the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} | etymology = | country = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | subdivision1 = {{hlist|{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}}|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano}}}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma}} | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a {{ill|Mountain range|en|Mountain range|WD=Q46831|lt=Mountain range}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano}} and {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}} prefectures in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the {{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains}} ("Northern Alps") and the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the {{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps}}. == Outline == The mountain range consists of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}}. The {{ill|Komagatake Ropeway|en|Komagatake Ropeway|WD=Q6428059|lt=Komagatake Ropeway}} is on the east side of {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the {{ill|tree line|en|tree line|WD=Q207762|lt=tree line}}, and a lot of {{ill|alpine plant|en|alpine plant|WD=Q2582662|lt=alpine plant}}s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''{{ill|Leontopodium|en|Leontopodium|WD=Q1483297|lt=Leontopodium}}'' is {{ill|Endemism|en|Endemism|WD=Q123452|lt=endemic}} around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
! class="unsortable" style="background-color:#ddd"|image

{{Convert|2291|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *[[Mount Nagiso]] (南木曽岳), {{Convert|1677|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Kiso) (風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== {{ill|River|en|River|WD=Q4022|lt=River}}s with [[:en:headwaters|headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to {{ill|Ise Bay|en|Ise Bay|WD=Q1368308|lt=Ise Bay}} of the {{ill|Pacific Ocean|en|Pacific Ocean|WD=Q98|lt=Pacific Ocean}}. They include: * {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|WD=Q779607|lt=Kiso River}} * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ({{ill|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|en|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|WD=Q905588|lt=Mount Amida}}) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|{{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mt. Kisokoma}} and Mt. Hōken seen from {{ill|Mount Sannosawa|en|Mount Sannosawa|WD=Q6923468|lt=Mount Sannosawa}} File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *{{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps}} **{{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains}} (Northern Alps) **{{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} (Southern Alps) *{{ill|List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|en|List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|WD=Q467843|lt=List of mountains in Japan}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] {{wikidata link|Q1072062}} [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the {{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains}} ("Northern Alps") and the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the {{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps}}. == Outline == The mountain range consists of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}}. comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1072062]] Wikidata name: Kiso Mountains [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from {{ill|Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|en|Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|WD=Q3695490|lt=Mount Senjō}} in the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} | etymology = | country = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | subdivision1 = {{hlist|{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}}|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano}}}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma}} | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a {{ill|Mountain range|en|Mountain range|WD=Q46831|lt=Mountain range}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano}} and {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}} prefectures in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the {{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains}} ("Northern Alps") and the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the {{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps}}. == Outline == The mountain range consists of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}}. The {{ill|Komagatake Ropeway|en|Komagatake Ropeway|WD=Q6428059|lt=Komagatake Ropeway}} is on the east side of {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the {{ill|tree line|en|tree line|WD=Q207762|lt=tree line}}, and a lot of {{ill|alpine plant|en|alpine plant|WD=Q2582662|lt=alpine plant}}s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''{{ill|Leontopodium|en|Leontopodium|WD=Q1483297|lt=Leontopodium}}'' is {{ill|Endemism|en|Endemism|WD=Q123452|lt=endemic}} around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

Nagano|Kiso District]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a {{ill|Mountain range|en|Mountain range|WD=Q46831|lt=Mountain range}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano}} and {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}} prefectures in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the {{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains}} ("Northern Alps") and the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the {{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps}}. == Outline == The mountain range consists of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}}. The {{ill|Komagatake Ropeway|en|Komagatake Ropeway|WD=Q6428059|lt=Komagatake Ropeway}} is on the east side of {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the {{ill|tree line|en|tree line|WD=Q207762|lt=tree line}}, and a lot of {{ill|alpine plant|en|alpine plant|WD=Q2582662|lt=alpine plant}}s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''{{ill|Leontopodium|en|Leontopodium|WD=Q1483297|lt=Leontopodium}}'' is {{ill|Endemism|en|Endemism|WD=Q123452|lt=endemic}} around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *{{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps}} **{{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains}} (Northern Alps) **{{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} (Southern Alps) *{{ill|List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|en|List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|WD=Q467843|lt=List of mountains in Japan}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] {{wikidata link|Q1072062}} [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

Кісо]][[zh:木曾山脈]] {{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from {{ill|Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|en|Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|WD=Q3695490|lt=Mount Senjō}} in the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} | etymology = | country = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | subdivision1 = {{hlist|{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}}|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano}}}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma}} | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a {{ill|Mountain range|en|Mountain range|WD=Q46831|lt=Mountain range}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano}} and {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}} prefectures in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the {{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains}} ("Northern Alps") and the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains}} ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the {{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps}}. == Outline == The mountain range consists of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}}. The {{ill|Komagatake Ropeway|en|Komagatake Ropeway|WD=Q6428059|lt=Komagatake Ropeway}} is on the east side of {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the {{ill|tree line|en|tree line|WD=Q207762|lt=tree line}}, and a lot of {{ill|alpine plant|en|alpine plant|WD=Q2582662|lt=alpine plant}}s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''{{ill|Leontopodium|en|Leontopodium|WD=Q1483297|lt=Leontopodium}}'' is {{ill|Endemism|en|Endemism|WD=Q123452|lt=endemic}} around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

{{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== {{ill|River|en|River|WD=Q4022|lt=River}}s with [[:en:headwaters|headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to {{ill|Ise Bay|en|Ise Bay|WD=Q1368308|lt=Ise Bay}} of the {{ill|Pacific Ocean|en|Pacific Ocean|WD=Q98|lt=Pacific Ocean}}. They include: * {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|WD=Q779607|lt=Kiso River}} * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ({{ill|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|en|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|WD=Q905588|lt=Mount Amida}}) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|{{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mt. Kisokoma}} and Mt. Hōken seen from {{ill|Mount Sannosawa|en|Mount Sannosawa|WD=Q6923468|lt=Mount Sannosawa}} File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Кісо]][[zh:木曾山脈]] {{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from {{ill|Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|en|Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|WD=Q3695490|lt=Mount Senjō|1=Mount Senjō (Akaishi)}} in the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains|1=Akaishi Mountains}} | etymology = | country = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | subdivision1 = {{hlist|{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu Prefecture}}|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano|1=Nagano Prefecture}}}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma|1=Mount Kisokoma}} | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a {{ill|Mountain range|en|Mountain range|WD=Q46831|lt=Mountain range|1=Mountain range}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano|1=Nagano Prefecture}} and {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu Prefecture}} prefectures in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the {{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains|1=Hida Mountains}} ("Northern Alps") and the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains|1=Akaishi Mountains}} ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the {{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps|1=Japanese Alps}}. == Outline == The mountain range consists of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}}. The {{ill|Komagatake Ropeway|en|Komagatake Ropeway|WD=Q6428059|lt=Komagatake Ropeway|1=Komagatake Ropeway}} is on the east side of {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma|1=Mount Kisokoma}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the {{ill|tree line|en|tree line|WD=Q207762|lt=tree line|1=Tree line}}, and a lot of {{ill|alpine plant|en|alpine plant|WD=Q2582662|lt=alpine plant|1=Alpine plant}}s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''{{ill|Leontopodium|en|Leontopodium|WD=Q1483297|lt=Leontopodium|1=Leontopodium}}'' is {{ill|Endemism|en|Endemism|WD=Q123452|lt=endemic|1=Endemism}} around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

Senjō (Akaishi)|WD=Q3695490|lt=Mount Senjō|1=Mount Senjō (Akaishi)}} in the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains|1=Akaishi Mountains}} | etymology = | country = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | subdivision1 = {{hlist|{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu Prefecture}}|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano|1=Nagano Prefecture}}}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma|1=Mount Kisokoma}} | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a {{ill|Mountain range|en|Mountain range|WD=Q46831|lt=Mountain range|1=Mountain range}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano|1=Nagano Prefecture}} and {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu Prefecture}} prefectures in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the {{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains|1=Hida Mountains}} ("Northern Alps") and the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains|1=Akaishi Mountains}} ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the {{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps|1=Japanese Alps}}. == Outline == The mountain range consists of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}}. The {{ill|Komagatake Ropeway|en|Komagatake Ropeway|WD=Q6428059|lt=Komagatake Ropeway|1=Komagatake Ropeway}} is on the east side of {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma|1=Mount Kisokoma}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the {{ill|tree line|en|tree line|WD=Q207762|lt=tree line|1=Tree line}}, and a lot of {{ill|alpine plant|en|alpine plant|WD=Q2582662|lt=alpine plant|1=Alpine plant}}s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''{{ill|Leontopodium|en|Leontopodium|WD=Q1483297|lt=Leontopodium|1=Leontopodium}}'' is {{ill|Endemism|en|Endemism|WD=Q123452|lt=endemic|1=Endemism}} around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

{{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a {{ill|Mountain range|en|Mountain range|WD=Q46831|lt=Mountain range|1=Mountain range}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano|1=Nagano Prefecture}} and {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu Prefecture}} prefectures in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the {{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains|1=Hida Mountains}} ("Northern Alps") and the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains|1=Akaishi Mountains}} ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the {{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps|1=Japanese Alps}}. == Outline == The mountain range consists of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}}. The {{ill|Komagatake Ropeway|en|Komagatake Ropeway|WD=Q6428059|lt=Komagatake Ropeway|1=Komagatake Ropeway}} is on the east side of {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma|1=Mount Kisokoma}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the {{ill|tree line|en|tree line|WD=Q207762|lt=tree line|1=Tree line}}, and a lot of {{ill|alpine plant|en|alpine plant|WD=Q2582662|lt=alpine plant|1=Alpine plant}}s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''{{ill|Leontopodium|en|Leontopodium|WD=Q1483297|lt=Leontopodium|1=Leontopodium}}'' is {{ill|Endemism|en|Endemism|WD=Q123452|lt=endemic|1=Endemism}} around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.

(風越山), {{Convert|1699|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} *Mount Kazakoshi (Ena) (風越山), {{Convert|1535|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} === Rivers=== {{ill|River|en|River|WD=Q4022|lt=River|1=River}}s with [[:en:headwaters|headwaters]] in the Kiso Mountains drain to {{ill|Ise Bay|en|Ise Bay|WD=Q1368308|lt=Ise Bay|1=Ise Bay}} of the {{ill|Pacific Ocean|en|Pacific Ocean|WD=Q98|lt=Pacific Ocean|1=Pacific Ocean}}. They include: * {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|WD=Q779607|lt=Kiso River|1=Kiso River}} * [[Tenryū River]] File:Kiso Mountains from Mt.Amidadake.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from east ({{ill|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|en|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|WD=Q905588|lt=Mount Amida|1=Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group}}) File:Cyuo-Alps from Kohideyama wt 2002-10-30.jpg|Kiso Mountains seen from west (Mount Kohide) File:Mount Kisokoma from Mount Sannosawa 1996-09-08.jpg|{{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|WD=Q3695791|lt=Mt. Kisokoma|1=Mount Kisokoma}} and Mt. Hōken seen from {{ill|Mount Sannosawa|en|Mount Sannosawa|WD=Q6923468|lt=Mount Sannosawa|1=Mount Sannosawa}} File:Mount Hoken from Mount Kazakoshi 2010-4-25.jpg|Mt. Kisokoma and Mt. Hōken seen from Mount Kazakoshi ==See also== *{{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|WD=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps|1=Japanese Alps}} **{{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|WD=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains|1=Hida Mountains}} (Northern Alps) **{{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|WD=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains|1=Akaishi Mountains}} (Southern Alps) *{{ill|List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|en|List of mountains and hills of Japan by height|WD=Q467843|lt=List of mountains in Japan|1=List of mountains and hills of Japan by height}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikivoyage|Chuo Alps}} {{commons Category-inline|Kiso Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Gifu-geo-stub}} [[en:Kiso Mountains]] [[arz:جبال كيسو]] [[bg:Кисо (хребет)]] [[ca:Muntanyes Kiso]] [[ckb:چیاکانی کیسۆ]] [[de:Kiso-Gebirge]] [[eo:Kiso montaro]] [[es:Cordillera Kiso]] [[eu:Kiso mendikatea]] [[fa:کوه‌های کیسو]] [[fr:Monts Kiso]] [[he:הרי קיסו]] [[it:Monti Kiso]] [[ja:木曽山脈]] [[ka:კისოს მთები]] [[ko:기소산맥]] [[lld:Crëps de Kiso]] [[lt:Kiso kalnai]] [[nl:Kisogebergte]] [[pl:Góry Kiso]] [[pt:Montanhas de Kiso]] [[ru:Кисо (хребет)]] [[tr:Kiso Dağları]] [[uk:Гори Кісо]] [[zh:木曾山脈]] {{wikidata link|Q1072062}} [[Category:Japan Alps]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains]] [[Category:Kiso Mountains| ]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Кісо]][[zh:木曾山脈]] {{Short description|Mountain range in the central part of the Japanese alps}} {{Infobox mountain| | name = Kiso Mountains | native_name = {{native name list |tag1=ja|name1=木曽山脈 |tag2=ja|name2=中央アルプス}} | other_name = Central Alps | photo = Kiso Mountains from Mount Senjo 2004-1-4.jpg | photo_size = | photo_caption = Kiso Mountains from {{ill|Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|en|Mount Senjō (Akaishi)|qid=Q3695490|lt=Mount Senjō|1=Mount Senjō (Akaishi)}} in the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|qid=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains|1=Akaishi Mountains}} | etymology = | country = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | subdivision1 = {{hlist|{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu Prefecture}}|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano|1=Nagano Prefecture}}}} | subdivision1_type = Prefectures | geology = | orogeny = | area_km2 = | range_coordinates = | length_km = 65 | length_orientation= | width_km = 15 | width_orientation = | highest = {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|qid=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma|1=Mount Kisokoma}} | highest_location = [[Kiso District, Nagano|Kiso District]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} | elevation_m = 2956 | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|22|N|137|48|16|E|type:mountain|format=dms|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiso Mountains'''|木曽山脈|Kiso Sanmyaku}} are a {{ill|Mountain range|en|Mountain range|qid=Q46831|lt=Mountain range|1=Mountain range}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano|1=Nagano Prefecture}} and {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu Prefecture}} prefectures in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. They are also called the {{nihongo|'''Central Alps'''|中央アルプス|Chūō Arupusu}} and they combine with the {{ill|Hida Mountains|en|Hida Mountains|qid=Q1072070|lt=Hida Mountains|1=Hida Mountains}} ("Northern Alps") and the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|qid=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains|1=Akaishi Mountains}} ("Southern Alps") to form a group collectively known as the {{ill|Japanese Alps|en|Japanese Alps|qid=Q645773|lt=Japanese Alps|1=Japanese Alps}}. == Outline == The mountain range consists of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|qid=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}}. The {{ill|Komagatake Ropeway|en|Komagatake Ropeway|qid=Q6428059|lt=Komagatake Ropeway|1=Komagatake Ropeway}} is on the east side of {{ill|Mount Kisokoma|en|Mount Kisokoma|qid=Q3695791|lt=Mount Kisokoma|1=Mount Kisokoma}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |title=Central Alps Komagatake Ropeway & Bus Services |publisher=Chuo Alps Kanko |access-date=2011-04-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101016025409/http://www.chuo-alps.com/en/ropeway.html |archive-date=2010-10-16 }} A lot of tourists visit the station on the top. The upper part of the mountain range is the {{ill|tree line|en|tree line|qid=Q207762|lt=tree line|1=Tree line}}, and a lot of {{ill|alpine plant|en|alpine plant|qid=Q2582662|lt=alpine plant|1=Alpine plant}}s grow naturally. Leontopodium shinanense of ''{{ill|Leontopodium|en|Leontopodium|qid=Q1483297|lt=Leontopodium|1=Leontopodium}}'' is {{ill|Endemism|en|Endemism|qid=Q123452|lt=endemic|1=Endemism}} around Mount Kisokoma. == Geography == === Major peaks === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! style="background-color:#ddd"|No.
